greatControversy: This passage stresses that the sanctuary and the investigative judgment are foundational truths that must be clearly understood by God's people. It declares that the sanctuary in heaven is the very centre of Christ's work for humanity, revealing the plan of salvation and the final triumph over sin. Every individual has a personal case before the judgment bar of God, making it essential for each person to study these truths for themselves.
hebrews: This passage presents Jesus as a great High Priest who has ascended to heaven. Because He has experienced human temptation yet remained without sin, He is able to fully sympathize with our weaknesses. Therefore, believers are encouraged to approach God's throne of grace with confidence, knowing they will receive mercy and find grace to help them in their time of need.
christInSanctuary: The earthly sanctuary and its services were a miniature representation of the true sanctuary in heaven where Christ ministers as our great High Priest. The daily sacrifices pointed forward to Christ's ultimate sacrifice on the cross. In the heavenly sanctuary, after His ascension, Christ entered the Most Holy Place to plead His own blood on behalf of repentant sinners and to present their prayers, perfumed with His righteousness, before the Father. This ongoing ministry assures us of His constant intercession, providing both pardon and the final cleansing from sin.

shareOptions.quote: If we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
And he said to me, 'For 2,300 days; then the sanctuary shall be cleansed.'
The subject of the sanctuary and the investigative judgment should be clearly understood by the people of God... The sanctuary in heaven is the very centre of Christ's work in behalf of men.
